text: Handling deadlines is a crucial skill in both academic and work settings. Good time management starts with breaking large tasks into smaller steps and setting personal deadlines ahead of the final due date. Prioritization helps you focus on what is most important first, rather than what is easiest or most enjoyable. Scope management involves clearly understanding the task requirements and avoiding adding extra work that was not requested. Finally, communication is key – if you feel a deadline is impossible, speak to your manager or teacher early, explain the situation, and propose a realistic alternative or request help.
